purpose: Formal conformance substrate for revocation-ref-v1. An external implementer who reproduces revocation_ref byte-identical from the stated revocation_artifact has a conformant implementation. The revocation is itself a trail action with its own action_ref, independent of the revocation_ref.

vectors.description: giskard-self revokes the delegation granted to pioneer-agent-001 (action_ref 584bc79b...) because the credential expired. The revocation is itself a trail action (action_type: authorization.revoke) with its own action_ref. The original delegation trail record is NOT mutated — the revocation is an append-only event.

chain.note: Verifier can walk: delegation trail record (action_ref=584bc7...) → revocation trail record (action_ref=fb4ef9..., revocation_ref=50cb4f...) → revocation_artifact. The chain is self-contained — no external lookups required.

invariants.revocation_ref_not_in_preimage: revocation_ref does not appear in revocation_action_preimage — revocation_action_ref determined solely by action_type, agent_id, scope, timestamp
invariants.append_only: The original delegation trail record (action_ref=584bc7...) is NOT mutated. The revocation creates a new trail record.
invariants.revoked_action_ref_links_back: revoked_action_ref in the artifact equals the action_ref of the delegated action trail record — enabling verifier chain traversal without external lookups

notes.revocation_key: Client-generated to ensure uniqueness of the revocation artifact. Without it, identical revocations would produce the same revocation_ref.
notes.no_retroactive_invalidation: A COMMITTED trail record using the revoked delegation is still a valid historical record. Revocation applies to future use only.
notes.revocation_check_at_ms: See action-ref.md — dual-timestamps close the gap between 'was valid when I last checked' and 'became invalid at this exact moment'.
